rulings.Case Title: ELLA CAVANAUGH et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANTS THE FALK CORPORATION AND REXNORD INDUSTRIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Moving parties were dismissed on June 24, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELLA CAVANAUGH et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has failed to meet the requirement of Aguilar that defendant demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to reasonably obtain evidence needed to establish liability. Defendant may not rely on plaintiff's answers to special interrogatories that were served with the reply. Defendant is limited to the evidence presented in the moving papers. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MICHAEL O'HALLORAN VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to July 28, 2008 on the Courts' own motion. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MICHAEL O'HALLORAN VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT PNUEMO ABEX, L L C'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. The supplemental interrogatory responses are admitted. The omission in the moving papers was due to clerical error and plaintiff was not prejudiced for the moving papers repeatedly disclosed defendants reliance on the responses. Defendant has shifted the burden of production based on the interrogatory responses and plaintiffs' deposition testimony. Plaintiff has not presented evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's objections to Kentfield declaration numbers 7, 8 and 9 are sustained. Defendant's objection to Jackson's declaration is overruled. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ZETTNER et al VS. BUFFALO PUMPS INC(SUED INDIVIDULLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT JOY TECHNOLOGIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof. Plaintiff's deposition testimony establishes exposure to defendant's product containing asbestos and the record does not reflect that Dr. Sandler considered all of plaintiff's testimony. Furthermore, Dr. Horn's declaration creates a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ARLO R HAUPT VS. A W CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof. Arlo Haupt described in detail contact with defendant's product; defendant made products with asbestos and has failed to demonstrate that the products described by plaintiff do not contain asbestos. =(302/PJM/xJL)

rulings.Case Title: PROPERTY MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS, INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. JOEL REIDY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 11, 2008, line 9. PLAINTIFF HORNEFF WADDLE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff concedes this is a motion for summary adjudication. However, such a motion was not noticed and the Court may not consider plaintiff's motion. Gonzales v. Superior Court (1987) 189 Cal. App. 3d 1542. =(302/PJM/PB)
